rows.notes: Clear centered page heading at top. Ink and lettering legible; no amounts on this line.

rows.description: Received by Bill drawn on Mr Bonny

rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt on a bill of exchange drawn on Mr Bonny, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Number '8' visible in pounds column with zeros in shillings/pence. Minor uncertainty only in reading of spelling of name; numeric alignment is clear.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Plate money paid by Mr Ratcliffe, amounting to £8 0s 0d.

rows.description: Received for an old Grate in ye Lumber Room
rows.notes: Pounds column shows an '8' aligned with other entries. Minor ambiguity in whether small marks to right are decoration or zeros; interpreted as 0 shillings and 0 pence based on column alignment.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt for the sale of an old grate from the lumber room, amounting to £8 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Right column shows '8' in pounds and '6' in shillings. The pence column appears blank. Some ink blur near the shillings digit makes it slightly ambiguous (could be 6 or 0), but alignment with neighbouring rows supports 6 shillings.
rows.english_description: Plate money paid by Mr Travanior (contribution toward the college plate fund)
rows.english_description_with_amount: Plate money paid by Mr Travanior, amounting to £8 6s 0d.

rows.description: Received of mr Bonny in full for the year to X'mas 1752
rows.notes: Pounds '50' is clear at right; shillings/pence blank. Description is slightly crowded but readable.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment received from Mr Bonny in full settlement for the year ending at Christmas 1752, amounting to £50 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Page shows a short label under the small grouped entries (appears to be 'Receptra' or 'Receipts'). The lettering is cramped and partly obscured by a pen flourish; assigned as header because it introduces the small totals block.

Three-part number at the right roughly reads 166 | 6 | 6. Vertical alignment used to determine column breaks. Ink smudge on the hundreds digit makes the hundreds/tens borderline but 166 is the best reading.
rows.english_description: Amount due from the previous year (outstanding debt carried forward)
rows.english_description_with_amount: Amount due from the previous year (outstanding debt carried forward), amounting to £166 6s 6d.

rows.notes: There is a stacked number to the right (second of three in the small totals) that reads approximately 553 | 3 | 6. The small heading text to left is indistinct; recorded as an entry because it appears as a line item in the totals block. Confidence low due to faint ink and cramped numbers.
rows.english_description: Unclear — possible transcription error (illegible short label)
rows.english_description_with_amount: Unclear — possible transcription error (illegible short label), amounting to £553 3s 6d.

rows.notes: Third stacked number in the small totals area appears approx. 323 | 12 | 4. There is some overwriting and a curved bracket near these numbers; alignment used to separate columns. Shillings value clearly two digits; pence digit faint but read as 4.
rows.english_description: Unclear — possible transcription error (illegible short label)
rows.english_description_with_amount: Unclear — possible transcription error (illegible short label), amounting to £323 12s 4d.

rows.description: Summa totalis Receptorum & Dd. An. praeced.
rows.notes: Large total at the right of the small block reads '1043' in the pounds column; shillings appears '1' and pence '8'. The line above and bracket marks indicate this is the sum. Some pen flourish and shadowing reduce clarity slightly.
Clear centered section heading separating the bottom half of the page. Lettering legible and purpose as a header evident.
Numbers read distinctly as 3 | 16 | 8 aligned with the pounds/shillings/pence ruling lines. Clear handwriting and spacing.
rows.english_description: Payment made to the City of Oxford
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment made to the City of Oxford, amounting to £3 16s 8d.

rows.notes: Short label (appears 'Coll. Ad' with abbreviation) followed by small amount aligned as 0 | 0 | 4. The tiny '4' in pence is clear; the descriptive abbreviation is somewhat unclear but treated as entry text exactly as written.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Small payment or collection (abbreviated label illegible), amounting to £0 0s 4d.
